title: Preparing an Overnight Culture
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7cq0m","text":"Plasmids can carry one or more antibiotic resistance genes, which confer resistance to a specific antibiotic to the bacteria carrying them. The presence of an antibiotic resistance gene on a plasmid allows researchers to easily isolate bacteria containing that plasmid from bacteria that do not contain it by artificial selection (i.e., growing the bacteria in the presence of the antibiotic).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0lel","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ng1c","text":"Luria broth (LB) is a nutrient-rich media commonly used to culture bacteria in the lab. LB agar plates are frequently used to isolate individual (clonal) colonies of bacteria carrying a specific plasmid. However, a liquid culture is capable of supporting a higher density of bacteria and is used to grow up sufficient numbers of bacteria necessary to isolate enough plasmid DNA for experimental use. The following protocol is for inoculating an overnight culture of liquid LB with bacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ao7pi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56reg","text":"https://www.addgene.org/protocols/pouring-lb-agar-plates/","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":57}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5E575B1E1DB311F186010A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.addgene.org/protocols/pouring-lb-agar-plates"}}}}
steps: Using a sterile pipette tip, select a single colony from your LB agar plate or a glycerol stock. The glycerol stock should never thaw. Keep on ice but in the bubble of sterility.
Drop the tip into the liquid LB + antibiotic and swirl.
Loosely cover the culture with sterile aluminum foil or the cap that is not air tight.
Incubate bacterial culture at 37 °C for 12–18 h in a shaking incubator
creator: Tamra Mbeuh Lahom Lot
